reasoning: The forecast date is March 16, 2025 (a Sunday), so the target threshold defaults to the closing price from Friday, March 14, which was exactly 126.11. The stock has shown strong local momentum, rising from ~122 to 126.11 over the preceding week. Because of this short-term upward momentum, the short-term probability (1 week out) of being higher than 126.11 is around 54%. However, on April 1, 2025, CAH has its ex-dividend date, which will mechanically reduce the share price by its $0.5056 dividend amount. This will slightly pull down the probability of remaining strictly above the current threshold for the April 15 resolution date. Moving to the longer-term resolution dates (June 2025 through March 2026), the initial momentum signal is entirely diluted by standard market volatility. While equities generally have a positive drift over a 1-year horizon, the regular quarterly dividend payments (removing ~$2.02 effectively over the year) negate roughly 1.6% of this growth when considering unadjusted share price. Abiding by the rules to increase uncertainty (push toward 0.5) over longer horizons, the probabilities smoothly curve down from 0.54 at 1 week out to 0.50 at the 1 year mark.
